seoTitle: How to Embed Typeform in an Email
metaDescription: See the best ways to share a Typeform in email campaigns using links, clickable buttons, and images that work across email providers.
intro: Collecting responses straight from an email campaign can boost engagement compared to sending people off-site with no context. This guide covers the practical ways to share a Typeform through email, since most email clients don't support fully embedded interactive forms, along with tips to improve click-through and conversions.

steps.heading: Email embedding options
steps.body: Understand that most email providers don't support a truly embedded interactive form, so sharing typically relies on links, buttons, or images that lead to the form.

steps.heading: Copy your Typeform link
steps.body: Get the shareable link for your form from Typeform's share settings so it can be added to your email content.

steps.heading: Add Typeform to your email
steps.body: Insert the link as a clickable button, text link, or image within your email template using your email marketing platform's editor.

steps.heading: Test and optimize your campaign
steps.body: Send a test email to check that the link works correctly, and track click-through and response rates to see how well the form performs.

faq.question: Can a Typeform be fully embedded inside an email?
faq.answer: Most email clients have limitations that prevent a fully interactive form from being embedded directly, so a link or button pointing to the form is the more reliable approach.

faq.question: What's the best way to increase clicks from an email to a Typeform?
faq.answer: Using a clear, prominent button rather than a plain text link tends to improve click-through, along with concise messaging about why the reader should respond.

faq.question: Can I track responses that come from an email campaign?
faq.answer: Yes, you can track responses by using a dedicated link or hidden fields to identify traffic coming specifically from your email campaign.

faq.question: Do all email providers support Typeform links the same way?
faq.answer: Different email clients have different rendering limitations, so it's worth testing your email across the providers your audience actually uses.
